signals.idea: Fix editor pane snap/jump: allocator should pre-compute coexistence height even when pane is hidden, so no height change occurs on visibility toggle

unresolved_flags.concern: The plan is built around a three-tier allocator, but the repository's current test contract already expects a different coexistence algorithm. Step 4 says the existing shrink-to-fit test should still pass, yet the checked-in test file expects `300/300` for the default 600px case, `200/400` when generations is 400px, and `156/94` in a 250px viewport. Current `getEffectivePaneHeights()` only implements the visibility short-circuit, ideal-fit, and fixed 70:30 split, so removing the visibility gate without first resolving whether the new tests are authoritative will likely codify the wrong behavior.

Step 4 understates which existing assertions change if heights are always precomputed. The provider exposes `effective*` heights even when both panes are hidden, so the initial-state test will also fail after this change; the plan only updates the solo-editor test and says the rest are unchanged. Following the plan as written would leave `PanesContext.test.tsx` inconsistent with the new behavior.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 2.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 2.

items: Toggling editor or generations pane visibility produces zero change in effectiveEditorPaneHeight and effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight
No visible snap/jump in LayoutMainContent padding when a pane toggles
Side-pane vertical centering (useBottomOffset) transitions smoothly without jumps
PaneControlTab slide animation uses correct target dimensions from the start
All existing PanesContext, LayoutMainContent, and useBottomOffset tests pass (updated as needed)
New test proves effective heights remain constant across visibility toggle

Step 4 says the existing shrink-to-fit test should still pass, yet the checked-in test file expects `300/300` for the default 600px case, `200/400` when generations is 400px, and `156/94` in a 250px viewport. Current `getEffectivePaneHeights()` only implements the visibility short-circuit, ideal-fit, and fixed 70:30 split, so removing the visibility gate without first resolving whether the new tests are authoritative will likely codify the wrong behavior.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx:87-126` implements only the visibility gate, ideal-fit, and fixed 70:30 split. `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx:221-289` expects equal split / preserve-generations / degraded-proportional outcomes that do not match that code.

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 4 understates which existing assertions change if heights are always precomputed. The provider exposes `effective*` heights even when both panes are hidden, so the initial-state test will also fail after this change; the plan only updates the solo-editor test and says the rest are unchanged. Following the plan as written would leave `PanesContext.test.tsx` inconsistent with the new behavior.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx:139-153` currently asserts initial hidden-state values of `effectiveGensHeight=300` and `effectiveEditorHeight=540`. With the visibility gate removed in `src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx:102-106`, those exported values will be pre-constrained even before either pane is opened.
